features.title: Faster expense entry + budget vs actual per day
features.what: Logging a spend is now near-instant: the quick-add form needs only an amount — the note is optional and category, who-paid, and 50/50-vs-payer-only split all default sensibly, so it's type-the-number-and-Add (≤3 taps). Underneath, the settle-up summary still spells out exactly who owes whom. And a new "Budget vs actual" card breaks the trip down day by day — each day's guide estimate next to what you've actually logged, with the difference coloured (terra = over, pine = under) and a running total at the top — so you can see at a glance which Alpine days ran hot or came in under.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 1cf8e9f on origin/main: new pure budgetVsActual(days,expenses) in core.js + Budget-vs-actual card wired in budget.js; the amount input is the only required field (title now optional). test-core exits 0 with 4 new budgetVsActual guards; pure-logic sanity check returned actual 120/delta 20/totals delta 20 as expected. Live core.js & budget.js served HTTP 200 with the budgetVsActual marker on first try (no Pages lag).

features.title: Nearby discovery into itinerary
features.what: Open any place in the itinerary and a new "Nearby eat & do" panel now suggests real spots around it — restaurants, cafés, bars, gelaterias, viewpoints, museums and attractions — pulled live from OpenStreetMap (free, no API key, cached for offline). They're ranked nearest-first, and one tap on a chip drops it straight into that day's plan with its category as a note. So when you're standing in an Alpine town wondering where to eat or what's worth a look, the options are one tap away instead of a separate map search.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge b19bf3d on origin/main (pure overpassUrl/parseOverpass/nearbyCacheKey in core.js; .pd-nearby drawer + data-near chips pushing via setPlan in itinerary.js); test-core exits 0 with 7 new B07 guards (named-only, deduped, nearest-first, amenity→food/tourism→act mapping); live core.js & itinerary.js serve HTTP 200 with the parseOverpass and pd-nearby markers after Pages propagation.

features.title: "Can I make it?" timing warnings
features.what: Your itinerary now sanity-checks the clock. Between consecutive stops, the travel-time connector compares the real driving/walking time against the gap you've actually scheduled — if the hop won't fit, it turns red and warns "⚠ tight by N min" so you know before you're standing there late. Whole days get checked too: if your stops plus travel blow past a sensible waking budget, a "⚠ Packed day" banner appears. On the seeded Alpine trip, the zero-buffer Sirmione → Jamaica Beach hop flags as tight while the relaxed afternoon stops stay quiet.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge f0bff42 on origin/main (pure legGapMins/legFeasibility/dayLoad in core.js, leg-tight + packed-day banner in itinerary.js); test-core exits 0 with new B06 guards; an independent import check confirmed a tight leg (90m travel vs 30m gap) → {tight:true} and a comfortable leg → {tight:false}; live core.js & itinerary.js serve HTTP 200 with the feature markers.

features.title: Unassigned-booking triage
features.what: No booking can silently fall through the cracks anymore. The 📥 Unassigned inbox in the bookings view now catches every booking that belongs to no trip — ones with no trip set, ones tagged 'unassigned', and ones still pointing at a trip you've since removed. Each gets a clear "→ assign to…" dropdown; pick a trip and it's filed instantly and remembered (saved to your bookings overlay).
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge bb862dc on origin/main (new pure orphanBookings in core.js, wired into bookings.js inbox); test-core exits 0 with 2 new B05 guards (flags unassigned/no-trip/unknown-trip, clean booking → none); live js/core.js serves HTTP 200 with the orphanBookings marker.

features.title: Booking gap & conflict detector
features.what: The bookings view now scans your trip and flags problems in a dismissible banner: bookings dated outside the trip, two bookings overlapping the same time window, and broken flight chains (you arrive at one airport but the next flight departs another — a missing connecting/return leg). Conservative by design — hotels and campervans never count as time clashes and clean multi-city itineraries stay silent, so the seeded Alpine & Iceland trips raise nothing.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 66f686f on origin/main (bookingWarnings in core.js, bk-warnings banner in bookings.js); test-core exits 0 with 6 new B04 guards; detector raises 0 warnings on real preexchange/alpine/iceland data and 1 on a deliberately broken booking; live core.js serves 200 with the marker.

features.title: Mobile "Today" view
features.what: A single, thumb-friendly screen for the road: today's date and weather, the day's ordered plan with times, and the next upcoming booking. It auto-opens when the trip's dates contain today, and is always reachable from the new Today tab (previewing day 1 before the trip starts).
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed: js/today.js + 'today' view registration + auto-select on origin/main, sw.js precaches it; test-core exits 0 with new pickTodayDay/nextBooking guards; live js/today.js serves HTTP 200 with the Today-view marker.

features.title: Offline map tiles + last-known weather
features.what: Map tiles you've already viewed are kept in a dedicated cache (surviving app updates) and re-served with no signal — blank gaps instead of broken images where uncached. The day forecast is persisted per location, so weather still renders offline from the last-known copy.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed: tc-tiles tile cache + OSM handling + weatherCacheKey present on origin/main; test-core exits 0 with new B02 guards; live sw.js serves HTTP 200 with the tc-tiles marker.

features.title: Offline PWA shell
features.what: Service worker + manifest cache the whole app shell and every trip's JSON, so the Alpine itinerary, map and Today view open with no signal.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed live: homepage, sw.js and manifest all serve HTTP 200; service worker registered; test-core green with new precache guards.
